subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, ADB, UN/OHCHR, Freedom House, Lowy, USIP) + international press; state media EXCLUDED. Extremely data-thin (~130k population); many factors are low-confidence inference.
Among the most climate-existentially-threatened states on earth (avg elevation <2m; ~66% land loss at +0.9m sea-level rise); bought land in Fiji for relocation. Uses the Australian dollar.

Standout drags: a 2022 judicial crisis (President Maamau attempted to deport a foreign High Court judge and suspended the Court of Appeal), a transactional 2019 pivot to China, and existential sea-level rise; the RERF sovereign fund (~330% of GDP) is a genuine buffer.

economic.notes: A fishing-fee-and-RERF-financed economy using the Australian dollar, with no own monetary policy.

fiscal_state.notes: Deficit ~22% of GDP in 2024 narrowing to ~15% in 2025 on a wage freeze, structurally financed by RERF withdrawals not own revenue. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: Uses the Australian dollar, no central bank - imports RBA policy entirely. (IMF; definitional)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: No own currency; the AUD is a foreign unit. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Imported via the AUD - elevated but moderating, with no domestic monetary driver. (IMF 2025)

banking_stability.notes: A tiny, shallow sector with little public supervision data. (inference; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A negligible export base - income is dominated by volatile fishing-license fees and aid, with chronic import dependence. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: A serious 2022 attack on judicial independence, with thin capacity and limited independent media.

rule_of_law.notes: The 2022 attack on the judiciary (a judge's deportation, suspension of the entire Court of Appeal) badly damaged independence and remains unresolved. (UN/OHCHR 2022; CIVICUS)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF-engaged with usable but very low-capacity, lagging statistics. (IMF Art IV)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A thin, remote, capacity-constrained administration spread across dispersed atolls. (World Bank; inference)

press_freedom.notes: A small, state-dominated media landscape with limited independent outlets; not separately RSF-ranked. (inference)

political_social.notes: Orderly elections undercut by rule-of-law backsliding, over a homogeneous but congested society.

political_stability.notes: Maamau won a third term (Oct 2024); orderly elections but recent executive-judiciary clashes and opposition concern over China opacity. (Al Jazeera; USIP 2024)

civil_liberties.notes: A broadly free society but with recent rule-of-law backsliding as a drag. (inference; Freedom House framing)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A small foreign population; the deportation of a long-resident foreign judge is a negative signal. (UN/OHCHR; inference)

social_cohesion.notes: A largely homogeneous I-Kiribati society, with the main fracture outer-island vs South Tarawa congestion/inequality. (inference)

demographics.notes: Young but poor and aid-dependent, with severe South Tarawa overcrowding and emigration pressure. (World Bank; inference)

geopolitical.notes: A transactional, opaque pivot to China, conflict-free but a contested square on the Pacific chessboard.

alliance_reliability.notes: Pivoted hard to China (2019), reducing predictability for Western partners - a transactional and opaque alignment. (USIP; Newsweek)

conflict_involvement.notes: No armed conflict; the risk is being a contested square on the US-China Pacific chessboard (Kanton), not active war. (Reuters; inference)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No sanctions; AUD use means open capital flows, with a tiny financial footprint. (inference)

physical_practical.notes: An existential sea-level threat, with very limited services across remote atolls.

climate.notes: A low-lying atoll nation - freshwater-lens salinization and inundation threaten habitability; bought land in Fiji as contingency. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Very limited services, with heavy reliance on overseas referral and donor (incl. recent Chinese) medical support. (inference; RNZ)

infrastructure.notes: Remote dispersed atolls with weak power/water/transport - donor-funded projects only. (World Bank; IMF)

crime_safety.notes: A low violent-crime baseline; the main risks are environmental, not crime. (inference)

disaster_insurance.notes: Acute, compounding sea-level/king-tide/drought hazard with negligible insurance; self-insures via RERF/aid. (inference; IMF)

summary: A 130k-person, 33-atoll micro-state facing one of the planet's clearest existential climate threats - average elevation under two metres, with relocation land already bought in Fiji. Its standout strength is unusual fiscal cushioning for its size: the Revenue Equalisation Reserve Fund (~330% of GDP) plus volatile fishing-license fees, though deficits run double-digit and only RERF drawdowns and aid keep it solvent. The 2022 judicial crisis - the attempted deportation of a foreign High Court judge and suspension of the entire Court of Appeal under President Maamau (re-elected in 2024) - inflicts the deepest institutional damage and drives the negative skew, compounded by a transactional pivot to China since 2019. The near-term outlook is poor-but-stable; the long-term outlook is dominated by sea-level rise.
